Name and CN #.4: TTrochlear CN 4  able continued  on next page

Exits Cranium Through.5: STTThe ophthalmic, maxillary and mandibular branches leave the skull through three separate foramina uperior orbital fissure he foramen rotundum  he foramen ovale

Site Code: Cranial Nerve.0: Within cranium, unknown which nerve C725
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.2: All portions are covered by meninges/dura so are reportable as C723
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.4: Arises from the dorsal brain stem, loops around the brainstem and passes anteriorly within the subarachnoid space. It travels between the superior cerebellar and posterior cerebral arteries and through the dura, enters cavernous sinus C725
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.5: CN5 originates in the pons. Upon leaving the pons it enters a small fossa posterior and inferolateral to the cavernous sinus called Meckel's (trigeminal) cave C725
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.6: Exits brainstem at junction of pons and the medulla, enters the subarachnoid space and runs upward between the pons and the clivus entering the cavernous sinus C725
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.7: CN7 originates in the pons, along the posterior cranial fossa (posterior cranial fossa (the posterior cranial fossa is part of the intracranial cavity) C725
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.8: BOriginates in the brain stem (medulla oblongata) between the base of the brain (pons) and the spinal cord C724 oth the vestibular branch and the cochlear branch are located in the inner ear
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.9: Originates in the anterior portion of the medulla oblongata C725
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.10: The vagus nerve originates from the medulla of the brainstem C725
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.11: The spinal accessory nerve originates in the neurons of the upper spinal cord, specifically C1-C5/C6 spinal nerve roots. The nerve enters the foramen magnum or lateral aspect of the medulla oblongata. The fibers of the spinal accessory nerve coalesce to form spinal rootlets, roots, and finally the spinal accessory nerve itself C725
Site Code: Cranial Nerve.12: CN12 starts in the hypoglossal nucleus of the brainstem, C725

Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.1: Originates on the olfactory mucosa of nasal cavity, then travels through the cribriform plate of the ethmoid bone C470
Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.3: After exiting the superior orbital fissure, the nerve enters the orbit C470
Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.5: • Ophthalmic nerve branch crosses the pterygopalatine fossa, inclines laterally on the back of the maxilla, and enters the orbit through the inferior orbital fissure where it is called the infraorbital nerve. It ends beneath the quadatus labii superius, and divides into multiple branches that spread to the side of the nose, the lower eyelid, and the upper lip C470 • Maxillary nerve leaves foramen rotundum and traverses the infraorbital groove and canal in the floor of the orbit, and appears on the face at the infraorbital foramen C470 • Mandibular nerve leaves via the foramen ovale travels along the mandibular groove C470
Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.6: Dorello's canal and travels to the tip of the temporal bone. Enters orbit C470
Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.7: Enters the temple through the internal auditory meatus and runs through the facial canal. C470
Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.9: Jugular foramen Between the internal jugular vein and internal carotid artery Lies on the stylopharyngeus and middle pharyngeal constrictor muscle Passes under the hypoglossus muscle Palatine tonsil Extends to mucous glands of the mouth, and base of the tongue C470
Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.10: CN10 descends within the carotid sheath medial to the internal jugular vein at the root of the neck C470  The right vagus crosses in front of the subclavian artery and travels into the fat behind the blood vessels, reaching the thorax. It then inclines behind the hilum of the right lung and moves toward the esophagus. The nerve splits into the right and left vagus at the esophageal plexus C473  Forms the anterior and posterior gastric nerves C475
Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.11: The nerve exits the skull through the jugular foramen. It then runs along the internal carotid artery within the neck C470 Reaches the sternocleidomastoid muscle and the trapezius C476
Site Code: Peripheral Nerve.12: CN12 exits the hypoglossal canal, traveling between the carotid artery and jugular vein, ending under the tongue C470
